rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MORALES et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION,LTD. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Lynn R. Fiorentino
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 28, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT CHRYSLER LLC'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH MICHAEL O'HALLORAN VS. AC AND S, INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 28, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff's objections to decedent's interrogatory answers are overruled for plaintiff is suing as decedent's successor in interest. Defendant has shifted the burden of producing evidence by reliance on decedent's interrogatory responses and the deposition testimony of plaintiff and decedent. This record reflects the inability of plaintiff to prove that decedent was exposed to asbestos from a product manufactured or supplied by defendant. Defendant's objection to the Newsock declaration is sustained. Defendant's objections to Ay's declaration appear well taken for the declaration lacks facts that establish that Ay is an expert on automotive repair work and/or any special knowledge as to defendant's products. Even assuming Ay's declaration is admissible, plaintiff fails to present evidence that creates a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: PRENTICE EARL SANDERS et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 28, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND TERENCE HALLINAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. A hearing is required. Judge Mahoney discloses that while at the City Attorney's Office from 1992 to 2000, he worked with Mr. Sanders in connection with the 101 California case and with Mr. Hallinan on various matters. In addition, deputies reporting to Judge Mahoney worked with both men during this period. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: 49 ZOE STREET HOMEOWERS'ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHARLES O.JONES CO.,INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 28, 2008, line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT MARK'S FURNACE SYSTEM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The Court takes judicial notice of the date plaintiff's corporate status was suspended, the date of occupancy and when the complaint was filed and the date of plaintiff's corporate reviver. The complaint is barred by the statute of limitations, CCP 337.15. The complaint was filed when plaintiff's corporate status was suspended and remained suspended when the limitations period expired. The statute of limitations is a substantive defense and the revival of plaintiff's corporate status is not entitled to retroactive application. Benton v. County of Napa (1991) 226 Cal. App. 3d 1485, 1490. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: 49 ZOE STREET HOMEOWERS'ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHARLES O.JONES CO.,INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 28, 2008, line 4. PLAINTIFF 49 ZOE STREET HOMEOWERS' ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The motion is denied. The proposed amendment would defeat the statute of limitations defense. As a consequence, the decision is Sade Shoe Co. v. Oschin & Snyder (1990) 217 Cal. App. 3d 1509, 1513, holds that the Court must deny the request for leave to amend. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: BERNARD ROGOVER VS. A. P. GREEN INDUSTRIES, INC. (A.P. GRREN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 28, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. A hearing is required to address whether defendant served Exhibit B, pages 704 - 705, with its moving papers. The courtesy copies do not contain the pages. If the pages were not served, the motion is granted if plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. Defendant shifted the burden of production. Plaintiff's discovery responses and deposition testimony are factually devoid. There does not appear to be a basis to admit the Bedros deposition and plaintiff's declaration appears to be subject to motion to strike under D'Amico. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: ARLO R HAUPT VS. A W CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 28, 2008, line 17. DEFENDANT INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence. Plaintiff presents evidence of a triable issue of fact based on the testimony of Mr. Mangus and the declarations of Mangus, Ay and Cohen. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to punitive damages. The motion is denied as to the intentional torts. Absent special interrogatories on these claims, the Court is unable to conclude that plaintiff cannot establish liability. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 28, 2008, line 6. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM QUAN VS. MICHAEL HAYNES et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 28, 2008, line 15. DEFENDANT MICHAEL HAYNES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 5 days to answer. Based on the allegations of the complaint, plaintiff is not entitled to notice under CCP 1161(1). The lease requires landlord approval for any sublet and the complaint alleges that no approval was given. Defendant has complied with S F A C 37.9 (a)(7). =(302/PJM/PB)
